task_desc: Fix facing direction oscillation causing visible fly body jitter at target heading -- the facingDir interpolation at main.js:1296-1299 uses a fixed turn step of 0.1*dtScale radians per frame regardless of the remaining angle to target; when abs(angleDiff) is smaller than 0.1*dtScale (~5.7 degrees at 60fps), the fly overshoots the target heading and reverses direction next frame, creating a sustained per-frame oscillation; tracing: if angleDiff=0.03, the fly turns 0.1 (overshoot to -0.07), next frame turns back 0.1 (overshoot to +0.03), repeating indefinitely at half the frame rate; this produces a ~5.7-degree peak-to-peak heading wobble visible through ctx.rotate(-facingDir + PI/2) at main.js:1441 as rapid body rotation jitter; most noticeable during slow or stationary states (idle, groom, rest, feed) where targetDir is stable but the fly body visibly vibrates; also causes slight zigzag during feed-state drift toward food. Fix: either (a) clamp the turn step to not exceed the remaining angle: replace the two branches at main.js:1296-1299 with a single line like facingDir -= Math.sign(angleDiff) * Math.min(0.1 * dtScale, Math.abs(angleDiff)), or (b) switch to exponential interpolation using shortest-arc angleDiff, e.g. facingDir += normalizeAngle(targetDir - facingDir) * (1 - Math.pow(0.9, dtScale)), matching the pattern already used for wingSpread/proboscisExtend in updateAnimForBehavior at main.js:630,637; option (b) also gives smoother, more natural turn dynamics (faster when far from target, slower when close). Files: js/main.js.
